scenario: Granite Bank runs Camunda 8.8 and a junior operator is reviewing the modification mode limits before working on a multi-instance body that holds 700 running child tokens on a single flow node instance. The operator needs the exact action types that the 500 token per operation cap applies to in this version of Operate.
question: Which modification action types does the 500 token per operation limit on a multi-instance body apply to?

options.text: The 500 token cap applies when cancelling or moving all tokens within a single flow node instance nested in a multi-instance body, which are the documented actions covered by the per-operation limit

The 500 token cap applies to every modification action in Operate including add token, edit variable, and add variable, so each kind of modification is capped at the same 500 token boundary per operation
The 500 token cap applies only to add token actions;  the documented limit was introduced to prevent operators from seeding too many extra tokens on the same multi-instance body at once
The 500 token cap applies only to edit variable actions on a multi-instance body;  each edit fans out to a child token and the limit bounds how many child copies can change at once

a.text: Correct. The docs say the 500 token per operation cap applies when cancelling or moving all tokens within a single flow node instance nested in a multi-instance body; those are the action types named under the maximum token modification limit.

b.text: Incorrect. The documented cap names cancelling or moving all tokens on a multi-instance scope; it does not state that every modification action including add token, edit variable, and add variable is capped at the same number.

c.text: Incorrect. The cap names cancelling or moving all tokens specifically, not add token actions, so the limit is not framed as a control on seeding additional tokens on the body.

d.text: Incorrect. The cap names cancelling or moving all tokens on a multi-instance flow node instance, not edit variable actions on a multi-instance body, so the scope of the limit is different.

explanation: The 500 token per operation limit is documented for cancelling or moving all tokens within a single flow node instance nested in a multi-instance body. Other modification action types are not listed under the same per-operation cap.
